qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: MCP Server Command Injection via .mcp.json Configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes path boundary checks bypass via symlink, TOCTOU, and hardlink in src/security/path.rs (check_symlink_escape). This finding is about command injection through MCP server configuration in src/tools/mcp/discovery.rs and transport.rs. Different root cause, different vulnerable code, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes path boundary checks bypass via symlink, TOCTOU, and hardlink in ZeptoClaw's src/security/path.rs (check_symlink_escape). The sole qualifying finding (mcp_server_command_injection) addresses command injection via MCP server configuration in a different code path (src/tools/mcp/discovery.rs, transport.rs). It does not identify the path validation bypass, symlink handling flaw, TOCTOU race, or hardlink alias bypass described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: missing/inadequate symlink resolution in check_symlink_escape (src/security/path.rs), TOCTOU between validate_path_in_workspace and I/O, and hardlink alias bypass
Finding mcp_server_command_injection root cause: unchecked command/args in .mcp.json passed to tokio::process::Command::new() (src/tools/mcp/discovery.rs, transport.rs)
No finding references src/security/path.rs, check_symlink_escape, validate_path_in_workspace, or the symlink/TOCTOU/hardlink bypass mechanisms
